column2: A company is concerned that someone could read a laptop's storage after stealing the device. Which workstation security option directly addresses this risk?
column5: Authentication controls normal sign-in, but drive encryption protects stored data even when an attacker has physical possession of the storage device.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield Encryption DataAtRest Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A password policy already permits several character types, but users still choose very short passwords. Which password consideration should be strengthened?
column5: Character variety does not compensate for a password that is too short. A longer password or passphrase increases the work required to guess it.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield Passwords Length Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why can requiring varied character types improve password complexity?
column4: It broadens the possible password combinations and discourages simple, single-pattern choices.
column5: Character types are one part of complexity. Length and unpredictability still matter; a familiar word with a predictable symbol added is not strong merely because it satisfies a category rule.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield Passwords Complexity CharacterTypes Source::Messer-v140

column2: One employee uses the same strong password for several services. Which password consideration is missing?
column5: A password can meet length and complexity rules yet still create risk through reuse. Unique passwords keep one compromised service from exposing the same credential elsewhere.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield Passwords Uniqueness Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which password-policy consideration determines how long a password remains valid before it must be changed?
column5: Password expiration changes the credential on an otherwise active account. Account expiration instead disables the account at a specified date.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic Passwords Expiration Policy Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which BIOS/UEFI password type should be configured to prevent unauthorized changes to firmware settings?
column5: This password protects configuration access. A firmware user password instead requires authentication before booting the computer.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic BIOS UEFI Firmware Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which BIOS/UEFI password type requires authentication before the workstation can boot?
column5: A pre-boot password does not encrypt stored data. If protection is required after someone removes the drive, data-at-rest encryption addresses that risk.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic BIOS UEFI BootSecurity Source::Messer-v140

column2: Employees sometimes step away from signed-in workstations without securing them. Which hardening option protects these unattended sessions automatically?
column4: A password-protected screen lock that activates after an inactivity timeout.
column5: The timeout limits the window in which an unattended authenticated session is exposed. Users should also lock the screen manually when stepping away.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield ScreenLock Timeout Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user has finished working on a shared workstation. Should the user lock the screen or log off?
column5: A lock is appropriate for a temporary absence because it preserves the user's session. Logging off ends the finished session so the next user cannot resume it under the previous identity.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic Logoff Sessions Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Company laptops are left unattended in an accessible office area. Which end-user hardening practice directly reduces the risk that a laptop is taken?
column4: Physically secure the laptop, such as with a suitable lock or secure storage.
column5: Encryption protects data if a device is stolen, but it does not prevent the hardware loss. Physical protection addresses the theft risk itself.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic PhysicalSecurity Laptops Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A fixed workstation displays PII where people passing in a hallway can see the screen, and the display cannot be relocated. Which protection directly addresses this exposure?
column5: This scenario involves visual disclosure from an authorized session. A screen lock protects the display after inactivity, but positioning or a privacy filter helps while the user is actively working.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic PII Privacy Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: Users reuse passwords because they cannot remember a different strong password for every account. Which security option best supports unique passwords?
column5: A password manager stores many credentials in one protected database, making unique passwords practical. Its own access credentials and recovery method must be secured carefully.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield PasswordManagers Passwords Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: An employee needs one business application but has administrator rights for all workstation tasks. Which account-management change best limits the impact of misuse or malicious software?
column4: Restrict the account to only the permissions required for the employee's work.
column5: This is least privilege. Unnecessary administrative rights expand what can be changed or accessed if the account is misused or compromised.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield LeastPrivilege Permissions Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A partner account should be usable only during an approved nightly support window. Which account control best enforces that requirement?
column5: Log-in time restrictions limit when authentication is allowed. Account expiration is different: it ends access on a date rather than defining a recurring time window.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic LoginTimes Accounts Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A workstation's guest account is not required. What should be done with it as part of account hardening?
column5: An unneeded account is another possible access path. Disabling it removes that path without granting ordinary users broader permissions.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield GuestAccount Accounts Hardening Source::Messer-v140

column2: Logs show repeated failed password guesses against the same workstation account. Which account control directly slows this online guessing attempt?
column5: Lockout stops further authentication attempts after a configured threshold. The threshold and recovery method should follow organizational policy rather than a universal value.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield AccountLockout BruteForce Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A contractor's workstation access must end automatically when the engagement ends. Which account setting should be configured?
column5: Account expiration disables use of the account after a date. Password expiration merely requires the active user to replace an aging password.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic AccountExpiration Accounts Scenario Source::Messer-v140

column2: A newly deployed workstation still uses its default administrator account name and password. What hardening change is required?
column4: Change both the default administrator account name and its password.
column5: Defaults can be widely known and give an attacker a predictable username and credential to target. Changing only the password still leaves a known account name.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield DefaultCredentials Administrator Hardening Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which workstation hardening setting prevents inserted media from automatically starting a program through its AutoRun instructions?
column5: AutoRun can initiate a program according to media instructions. AutoPlay is a related but separate feature that presents handling choices to the user.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield AutoRun RemovableMedia Hardening Source::Messer-v140

column2: A workstation is running a network-accessible service that no approved application uses. What hardening action reduces the related attack surface?
column5: An active service can provide code or an interface for an attacker to target. Confirm that it is truly unnecessary before disabling it so required functions and dependencies are not disrupted.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 2.7; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 43
column10: A+::220-1202::2.7 A+::220-1202::Domain2-Security A+::220-1202::WorkstationSecurityandHardening Basic HighYield Services AttackSurface Hardening Source::Messer-v140
